Ford CEO Farley on Supplier Fire, Tariff Impact and EVs
data.description:
Ford CEO (F) Jim Farley discusses the impact of a devastating fire at a key aluminum supplier on earnings, how the automaker is managing tariffs and the state of the electric-vehicle market. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." Ford now expects full-year adjusted earnings before interest and taxes of $6 billion to $6.5 billion, down from as much as $7.5 billion under its previous guidance.

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Baker Hughes beats profit estimates on strong industrial and energy tech demand
data.description:
(Reuters) -Oilfield services provider Baker Hughes on Thursday beat Wall Street estimates for third-quarter profit, driven by strength in its industrial and energy technology (IET) business.

As oilfield services firms navigate a volatile market, resilient areas like LNG infrastructure, power grid upgrades, and growing electricity demand from data centers are helping support growth.

Baker has been also leaning on its industrial and energy technology (IET) division to strengthen its footprint in the natural gas and LNG market, a strategy expected to underpin revenue growth and positioning in the energy transition.

The company said IET backlog rose 3% sequentially to a record $32.1 billion, while quarterly orders surged 44% year-on-year to $4.14 billion, reflecting growing demand for LNG and energy transition projects.

Baker Hughes expects IET revenue between $3.2 billion and $3.7 billion in the current quarter, and $12.8 billion to $13.3 billion for the full year, with annual orders seen at $13.5 billion to $14.5 billion.

"While oilfield services & equipment segment margins softened, reflecting the broader macro backdrop, IET delivered another quarter of strong performance, driving consolidated adjusted EBITDA margins higher year-over-year," said Baker Hughes CEO Lorenzo Simonelli.

Oilfield service providers are contending with subdued demand for traditional drilling and completion work as producers adopt more efficient extraction technologies and rising supplies curb the need for new wells.

Operators also remain cautious amid volatile oil prices, OPEC+ spare capacity, and trade uncertainties, particularly in North America.

The Houston-based company reported adjusted earnings of 68 cents per share for the three months ended September 30, topping analysts' average estimate of 62 cents, according to LSEG data.

Quarterly revenue of $7.01 billion also beat expectations of $6.83 billion.

Rivals SLB and Halliburton also topped third-quarter profit estimates.

(Reporting by Arunima Kumar in Bengaluru; Editing by Maju Samuel)

Utilities Flat on Cyclical Bias -- Utilities Roundup
data.description:
Shares of power producers were more or less flat as traders rotated into cyclical stocks, emboldened by reports of a planned meeting between President Trump and his Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping.

PG&E's third-quarter net income rose sharply as rising demand for electricity offset lower average residential rates and the company narrowed its core earnings projection for the year.

10-23-25 1738ET

data.title: Valero Still Plans to Shut Refinery After Talks with California Officials -- OPIS
data.description:
Valero Energy is proceeding with its plan to close its 145,000 b/d Benicia refinery in Northern California by next spring following talks with state officials about options to keep it running, the company's general counsel said on Thursday.

"We have been in discussions with California, but nothing has materialized out of that. And so as a result nothing has changed," Rich Walsh told analysts during a call to discuss the company's third-quarter financial results.

"Our plans are still moving forward as we have shared and as we have informed the state. So, I don't see anything changing on that," he said.

Valero said in April it would cease refining operations at the Benicia refinery by the end of April 2026.

Over the last several months, the California Energy Commission and state officials were reportedly working to find a buyer for the refinery and exploring other options to keep the facility in operation.

Phillips 66 said early this month its 156,000 b/d Los Angeles refinery received its final waterborne crude shipment and expected to halt crude processing in mid-October. The Valero and Phillips plants account for about 17% of California's crude oil refining capacity.

Valero Chief Executive Lane Riggs said on the call that the company will continue to supply fuel for its California wholesale operations after the refinery closes. He also said Valero plans to import waterborne barrels into the state from "anywhere in the world.

"We will figure out how to supply it," Riggs said.

He also said an FCC improvement project at its 235,000 b/d St. Charles, La., refinery will begin operating in the second half of next year. The $230 million project will allow the refinery to produce more high-value products, including high-octane alkylate.


This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.


-- Reporting by Frank Tang, ftang@opisnet.com; Editing by Jeff Barber, jbarber@opisnet.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1738ET

data.title: Communications Services Down After Mixed Earnings -- Communications Services Roundup
data.description:
Communications-services companies fell after mixed earnings.

T-Mobile US declined after its subscriber growth lagged Wall Street expectations. Rival AT&T was also on the retreat as traders digested its earnings.

Finnish telecom-services firm Nokia rose after the company's chief executive said robust demand from data-center customers associated with artificial-intelligence was set to continue.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1738ET

data.title: Tech Up on Trump-Xi Meeting Relief -- Tech Roundup
data.description:
Shares of technology companies rose amid relief following President Trump's reported arrangement to meet Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping at the APEC meeting in South Korea next Thursday.

Beijing's new five-year plan doubles down on its push for technology self-reliance, policy priorities that could further fuel its tensions with the U.S.

Tesla shares ticked up even after the electric car maker's earnings lagged some investors' expectations. Chief Executive Elon Musk pushed back on criticisms from major shareholder advisory groups of his proposed $1 trillion pay package.

Shares of quantum-computing firms including Rigetti Computing and IonQ surged after reports that the Trump administration was weighing investment in the producers of next-generation computing technology, possibly in the form of awards of $10 million or more.

Shares of AI darling Nvidia rose, testing all-time highs, as strategists debated the sustainability of the AI boom.

Intel shares rose in late trading after it reported sales growth in its core product line of PC processors and shrinking costs, signs its turnaround efforts are meeting some success.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1737ET

data.title: Financials Up on Hopes For Trade-War Subsiding -- Financials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of banks and other financial institutions rose slightly as fears about an escalation in the U.S.-China trade war subsided.

Blackstone shares fell after the alternative investment giant said quarterly net income fell despite a $54 billion inflow from investors.

President Trump has pardoned Changpeng Zhao, the convicted founder of the crypto exchange Binance, following months of efforts by Zhao to boost the Trump family's own crypto company.

Solmate Infrastructure shares rallied after the developer of infrastructure for cryptocurrency network Solana said it was hunting for a major acquisition.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1736ET

data.title: Consumer Cos Up After Strong Luxury, Travel Results -- Consumer Roundup
data.description:
Consumer companies rose amid strong sales for travel and luxury firms, and as President Trump's appointment to meet Chinese counterpart Xi Jinping at the meeting of the Asian trade group known as APEC in South Korea next week.

Kering shares rallied after the Gucci owner said sales at that premium brand surpassed targets, suggesting a turnaround under new Chief Executive Luca de Meo may be gaining traction.

Similarly, shares of Italian fashion house Prada rose after it posted 9% revenue growth, adjusted for currency movements.

American Airlines shares rose after the airline said growth in premium ticket sales continued to offset less profitable cabin ticket sales, echoing statements from rivals such as United Airlines.

Southwest Airlines fell as the discount airline's earnings were shy of some investors' expectations.

Intel's turnaround effort gathered momentum as it reported sales growth in its core product line of PC processors and shrinking costs, the result of cuts that have accelerated this year.

Used-home sales rose 1.5% from the prior month to a seasonally adjusted annual rate of 4.06 million, the highest level since February, the National Association of Realtors said.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1736ET

data.title: Health Care Flat After Mixed Earnings -- Health Care Roundup
data.description:
Health-care companies were more or less flat after mixed earnings.

Shares of Molina Healthcare tumbled after the California health insurer said unexpectedly high medical costs in its Marketplace plans ate into profits.

Many health insurers, including the nation's largest, UnitedHealth Group, have grappled with medical costs in recent quarters as more Americans avail of increasingly expensive procedures.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1735ET

data.title: Industrials Up After Strong Earnings -- Industrials Roundup
data.description:
Shares of industrial and transportation companies rose after a strong round of earnings.

Shares of industrial conglomerate Honeywell International rallied after it said third-quarter revenue rose 7% to $10.41 billion.

Dow's third-quarter profit fell as an ongoing petrochemical glut weighed on the prices of plastics and many of its other specialty material products.

The Federal Reserve Bank of Kansas City said its Manufacturing Survey's composite index climbed to 6 this month from 4 in September, marking a fourth straight month of expansion after more than three years in contraction.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1735ET

data.title: Uscb Financial Holdings Inc - reports EPS of $0.45 for Q3 2025 - SEC filing
AI data centre startup Crusoe raising $1.38 billion in latest funding round
data.description:
(Reuters) -AI data centre startup Crusoe is raising $1.38 billion at a valuation of about $10 billion from an anticipated Series E funding round, the Denver-based company said on Thursday

The oversubscribed round is being co-led by Valor Equity Partners and Mubadala Capital and includes Nvidia, Fidelity Management and Founders Fund as some of its major investors.

The company, which was involved in building OpenAI's first big data centre in the U.S., has raised about $3.9 billion since its inception in 2018.

It recently announced that the first phase of its 1.2 gigawatt data center in Abilene, Texas campus, was live just one year after construction began.

(Reporting by Anuj T in Bengaluru; Editing by Anil D'Silva)

Battery Startup Redwood Materials Tops $6 Billion Valuation
data.description:
Battery recycling startup Redwood Materials raises $350 million, pushing its valuation past $6 billion, according to sources. JB Straubel, CEO of Redwood Materials, joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech."

Bloomberg videos, provided by MT Newswires

data.title: Energy Up After Lukoil, Rosneft Sanctions -- Energy Roundup
data.description:
Shares of energy companies rose as U.S. sanctions on Russia caused a rally in crude.

Oil futures rose 5.6% to $61.79 a barrel after the U.S. tightened sanctions on Russian state-backed oil companies Lukoil and Rosneft.

Russia has managed to sell billions of dollars worth of oil in recent years despite the international sanctions that followed the invasion of Ukraine. The Trump administration is making a renewed effort to disrupt the export trade in an effort to push Vladimir Putin back to the negotiating table with Ukraine. In response, Putin reportedly said he would not bend to U.S. pressure.


Write to Rob Curran at rob.curran@dowjones.com

(END) Dow Jones Newswires

10-23-25 1733ET
